metadata.notes: Eval 1 and 3 without_skill runs failed due to API rate limit — those baselines are unavailable.

expectations.text: Agent reads agentFlow/flows/vsdd/instructions/behavioral-spec.md
expectations.evidence: Analysis quotes specific lines from instruction file

expectations.text: Agent reads agentFlow/tasks/navbar/behavioral-spec.md (the artifact)
expectations.evidence: Analysis references label prefixes, traceability table from artifact

expectations.text: Agent presents a numbered list with at least 3 optimization points
Agent explicitly mentions the revisionCount of 1 as a signal
expectations.evidence: revisionCount: 1 — a strong signal that the instructions caused at least one round-trip correction

expectations.text: Agent asks for approval before making any changes
No instruction files are modified during the run

notes: RATE LIMIT — run failed, results not meaningful for comparison
Eval 1 and 3 without_skill runs failed due to API rate limit — the +0.75 pass_rate delta is inflated by these failures
The only valid with_skill vs without_skill comparison is Eval 2 (not-done-step): with_skill 5/5 vs without_skill 2/5
Eval 2 is the most discriminating test: without the skill the agent ignored the revision state and produced analysis anyway — exactly the wrong behavior
All 3 with_skill runs passed 100% of assertions: consistent behavior across happy path, edge case, and clean-pass scenarios
The revisionCount=0 handling in Eval 3 is a subtle discriminating test that the skill passed correctly without explicit instruction to stay quiet
